typecheck-backend.note: No issues found in 12 source files

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Playwright Screenshot Workaround' section documenting that playwright___browser_take_screenshot writes to a temp dir and that page.screenshot({ path }) in playwright___browser_run_code is needed to write directly to the repo.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note in the skill description or preconditions that `frontend-worker` is intended for tasks involving Svelte/TypeScript/CSS code changes, NOT for pure markdown/documentation tasks. For doc-only tasks, the orchestrator should use a more general worker or create a doc-specific skill.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: readme-ypm-host-fix used frontend-worker for a pure README markdown change. Worker correctly deviated from Steps 4 (TDD/Vitest) and 6 (npm run build/svelte-check) — but the skill mismatch added noise to the transcript and required the worker to justify why frontend procedures didn't apply.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that the TDD requirement (Step 4, 'Write Tests First') applies to logic functions and stores, not to markup/CSS changes. For UI-only changes (HTML structure, CSS, Svelte template changes), verification via `svelte-check` + `npm run build` + browser inspection is the appropriate substitute.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both arbitrum-default-pair-bug and ui-improvements workers noted in skillFeedback that TDD was skipped for justified reasons (tiny fix in well-covered file; no component test harness for Svelte markup). The repo's test files confirm only pure-logic unit tests exist — no component rendering tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In Step 7 ('Manual Verification with agent-browser'), add a note that the built-in Playwright browser tools (playwright___browser_navigate, playwright___browser_snapshot, playwright___browser_run_code) are a valid substitute when agent-browser is unavailable. Also document the screenshot path workaround: use playwright___browser_run_code with page.screenshot({ path: '/abs/repo/path.png' }) to save screenshots directly to the repo (playwright___browser_take_screenshot writes to a temp dir).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: readme-screenshot-update worker could not use agent-browser and fell back to Playwright tools. Worker needed multiple attempts to locate the screenshot file before discovering the page.screenshot({ path }) workaround.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a CSS convention note: prefer scoped CSS classes for component-specific layout variants rather than broadening global selectors. Specifically, avoid changing global element selectors (like `label`) in app.css for changes that only apply to specific components. Use component-scoped or feature-class selectors instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ui-improvements changed the global `label` CSS selector from `display: block` to `display: flex` to support token field label layout. All other layout overrides in app.css use class selectors. This could unintentionally affect future labels with multi-word content or nested elements.

rejectedObservations.observation: README screenshot shows empty form state, not a completed quote result, which doesn't match the caption 'Latest-block USDC→crvUSD quote result with price age in seconds'.
rejectedObservations.reason: The feature description for readme-screenshot-update explicitly scoped the deliverable to 'showing updated layout (GitHub button, restructured token fields, clear buttons)'. The caption mismatch is pre-existing and out of scope for this milestone. If the caption matters, a separate task should update it.
